fixtures.name: paradigm_break_manual_dict_response
fixtures.rationale: Break axis: explicit field-by-field dict construction with manual float(), bool(), and .isoformat() coercions at every endpoint instead of response_model=. Canonical pattern: docs_src/response_model/tutorial001_py310.py:17-27 shows response_model=list[Item] and response_model=Item on every route with no manual coercions; corpus has 163 response_model= sites. OOV evidence: explicit float()/bool()/.isoformat() coercions at endpoint scope appear 0 times in the FastAPI corpus — type coercion is handled entirely by Pydantic in the idiomatic pattern. Single axis of deviation: absence of response_model= plus presence of per-field coercion idiom.

fixtures.name: paradigm_break_msgpack_response
fixtures.rationale: Break axis: msgpack.packb() binary serialization with Response(media_type='application/x-msgpack') at every endpoint instead of JSON/response_model=. Canonical pattern: docs_src/response_model/tutorial001_py310.py:17-27 uses response_model= for automatic JSON; corpus has 163 response_model= sites and 0 msgpack import sites. OOV evidence: msgpack is entirely absent from the FastAPI corpus (0 import sites) — all msgpack.* identifiers are OOV. Single axis of deviation: entire serialization pipeline replaced by binary msgpack, bypassing FastAPI's JSON stack.

fixtures.name: control_response_model_list
fixtures.rationale: Control: response_model=list[ItemOut] on the collection GET endpoint and response_model=ItemOut on single-item GET/POST, with response_model_exclude_unset=True on PATCH. Near-verbatim adaptation of docs_src/response_model/tutorial001_py310.py (lines 1-27) extended with CRUD operations. Corpus evidence: 163 response_model= sites confirm this as the dominant FastAPI serialization pattern. No manual type coercions, no Response(content=...) construction, no third-party serialization libraries. Demonstrates the list-variant of the response_model= axis to pair against manual_dict_response and msgpack_response breaks.
